title: Preparing Borax RapidBuffer for electrophoresis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"13grj","text":"Molecular biologists often use tris-based conduction buffers like TBE or TAE in running DNA gels. These buffers are known to overheat at high voltages, causing problems with gel integrity, sample denaturation and more. The voltage thus needs to be limited to a maximum of 5-10 volts/cm (e.g 100 volts for a 10 cm gel) leading to extended running time, sometimes to hours.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b23p0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ersu","text":"With 10mM Sodium Tetraborate (CAS#: 1330-43-4, Na2B4O7, Borax), the voltage can be increased to 35 volts/cm without any problem, reducing the time taken to run gels by up to 7 times for standard applications (e.g. separation of DNA fragments from 100 bp - 5 kbp).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":49,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":51,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":53,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m0bv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bk7lq","text":"Our lab’s standard DNA gel buffer has become 20 g of Borax into 1 l of water which gives a 20x stock solution. Small gels run in 10-15 minutes at 200V.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f6m5d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"83bra","text":" SCOPE: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fea2j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"edjqs","text":"Preparation of gel electrophoresis buffer for separation of DNA fragments from 100 bp - 5 kbp","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7cdgk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"981vq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"50gfn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"fuvjr","text":"Reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2voek","text":"Borax (Sodium Tetraborate, CAS#: 1330-43-4)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c9eaf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2hqjv","text":"Materials and Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3gj6q","text":"Beaker","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3adps","text":"Measuring cylinder","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"22edk","text":"Distilled water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"37v7m","text":"Container with lid","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"clbdb","text":"Weighing balance ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1olmp","text":"Weighing boat and spatula","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f3rcq","text":"Magnetic stirrer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Pour the prepared solution into a clean, labelled screw-top 1000 mL glass or plastic storage bottle and store at Room temperature until use.
Add distilled or de-ionised water to a total of 1000 mL and stir again for 00:03:00
Add a magnetic flea and use a magnetic stirrer at Room temperature to fully dissolve the borax powder.
Using a 1000 mL measuring cylinder, measure 800 mL of distilled or deionised water and add to the 1000 mL beaker.
Pour powder into a graduated 1000 mL glass beaker or flask
Use a spatula to measure out and weigh 10 g of Borax (Sodium Tetraborate, CAS#: 1330-43-4)
1X RapidBuffer solution is now ready for use and can be stored at Room temperature for use within one month.
Add approx 450 mL distilled or deionised water, sufficient to make up the volume to 500 mL
Use a 100 mL measuring cylinder to measure out 50 mL of 10X RapidBuffer into a 500 mL graduated beaker or flask.

Weigh out a certain amount of borate and place into a vacuum Tupperware half filled with silica beads. place the lid on top and cover tightly, then use the vacuum pump to extract air inside and allow to dry for 12:00:00 at 37 °C ). 1.2 Carefully pour it out into zip lock bags and seal to avoid it from absorbing moisture and store in cool dry place.
creator: Nadine Mowoh
